title.value: StepSearch: Igniting LLMs Search Ability via Step-Wise Proximal Policy Optimization
title.evidence: StepSearch: Igniting LLMs Search Ability via Step-Wise Proximal Policy Optimization

multihop_reasoning_challenge.value: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.
multihop_reasoning_challenge.evidence: Efficient multi-hop reasoning requires Large Language Models (LLMs) based agents to acquire high-value external knowledge iteratively.

sparse_reward_problem.value: The sparse rewards from global signal only lead to underperformance on complex, multi-hop QA.
sparse_reward_problem.evidence: Previous work has explored reinforcement learning (RL) to train LLMs to perform search-based document retrieval, achieving notable improvements in QA performance, but underperform on complex, multi-hop QA resulting from the sparse rewards from global signal only.

search_efficiency_problem.value: Existing RL-based search agents depend on coarse global rewards, lacking fine-grained supervision of intermediate queries and multi-step retrievals.
search_efficiency_problem.evidence: However, existing RL-based search agents depend on coarse global rewards, lacking fine-grained supervision of intermediate queries and multi-step retrievals—an approach inadequate for the dependencies inherent in complex multi-hop reasoning.

existing_method_performance.value: Existing methods exhibit poor out-of-domain generalization and performance degradation on complex tasks.
existing_method_performance.evidence: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) frameworks—such as ToolFormer, ToolKengPT, and related efforts—substantially boost performance in specialized, knowledge-intensive tasks but suffer from poor out-of-domain generalization.

framework_overview.value: StepSearch is a framework for search LLMs that trained with step-wise proximal policy optimization method, consisting of richer intermediate search rewards and token-level process supervision.
framework_overview.evidence: To address this gap in existing research, we introduce StepSearch, a framework for search LLMs that trained with step-wise proximal policy optimization method. It consists of richer and more detailed intermediate search rewards and token-level process supervision based on information gain and redundancy penalties to better guide each search step.

subproblem_decomposition.value: We construct a multi-turn Q&A dataset with subquestion-level search trajectories.
subproblem_decomposition.evidence: In this pilot study, we construct a multi-turn Q&A dataset with subquestion-level search trajectories.

search_keyword_generation.value: Each enhanced step question is reformulated into a set of N search queries to facilitate information retrieval.
search_keyword_generation.evidence: Each enhanced step question is then reformulated into a set of N search queries to facilitate information retrieval.

cross_engine_validation.value: Queries are issued to M sources, and only those returning valid results in at least ⌈M / 2⌉ sources are retained.
cross_engine_validation.evidence: Queries are issued to M sources (e.g., Google, Bing, Wiki-18), and only those returning valid results in at least ⌈M / 2⌉ sources are retained.

dataset_scale.value: We developed a novel MuSiQue-based pipeline, contributing 60k filtered sub-question search keywords that generalize across retrieval datasets.
dataset_scale.evidence: We develop a novel MuSiQue-based pipeline, contributing 60k filtered sub-question search keywords that generalize across retrieval datasets.

global_rewards.value: The global reward is based on final answer accuracy and keyword hit rate.
global_rewards.evidence: The global reward is set at the last position of the output, while the step-wise reward is set at the last token of each round of search behavior.

step_rewards.value: The step-wise reward is expressed as information gain minus redundancy penalty.
step_rewards.evidence: The step-wise reward r_{step}^{t} of each round of search behaviour can be expressed as information gain G^{t} minus redundancy penalty P^{t}.

algorithm_basis.value: Our optimization algorithm combines the above-mentioned Search Steps supervision reward based on the currently widely used actor-critic approach PPO.
algorithm_basis.evidence: Our optimization algorithm combines the above-mentioned Search Steps supervision reward based on the currently widely used actor-critic approach PPO.

core_mechanism.value: StepSearch augments PPO with token-level rewards that combine information gain and redundancy penalties.
core_mechanism.evidence: By augmenting PPO with token-level rewards that combine information gain and redundancy penalties, StepSearch boosts policy convergence and improves retrieval fidelity and QA accuracy.

mathematical_formulation.value: The policy π_{θ} is optimized with the reference policy π_{θ_{ref}} by maximizing the expected value based on the advantage A_{t}.
mathematical_formulation.evidence: For each sample input x ∼ D, obtain output o from the old policy π_{θ_{old}}, let I(y_{t}) be the token-loss masking indicator, it equals 1 when o_{t} is generated by actor LLM else 0 for retrieved tokens. Then optimize the policy π_{θ} with the reference policy π_{θ_{ref}} by maximizing the following objective.

performance_improvements.value: StepSearch significantly outperforms global-reward baselines, achieving 11.2% and 4.2% absolute improvements for 3B and 7B models.
performance_improvements.evidence: On standard multi-hop QA benchmarks, it significantly outperforms global-reward baselines, achieving 11.2% and 4.2% absolute improvements for 3B and 7B models over various search with RL baselines using only 19k training data.

data_efficiency.value: Using only 19k training data, StepSearch shows significant performance improvements.
data_efficiency.evidence: demonstrating the effectiveness of fine-grained, stepwise supervision in optimizing deep search LLMs.

generalization_capability.value: StepSearch shows higher adaptability to out-of-domain knowledge bases.
generalization_capability.evidence: StepSearch shows higher adaptability to out-of-domain knowledge bases.

ablation_study_findings.value: The step-wise token-level reward mechanism has more obvious advantages and stability compared to the global reward.
ablation_study_findings.evidence: The step-wise token-level reward mechanism (ow-ss) has more obvious advantages and stability compared to the global reward of search process (ow-skr).

key_innovations.value: StepSearch integrates iterative retrieval with explicit step-wise supervision for search LLMs.
key_innovations.evidence: To address these shortcomings, We propose StepSearch, a reinforcement learning framework that integrates iterative retrieval with explicit step-wise supervision for search LLMs.

method_comparison.value: StepSearch consistently outperforms strong baseline Search-RL methods.
method_comparison.evidence: StepSearch consistently outperforms strong baseline Search-RL methods.

case_study_example.value: In case studies, the model's performance improved significantly with the proposed framework.
case_study_example.evidence: We observe the sampling cases during the training process, and some of the sampling results are shown in the Table 6, 7.

current_limitations.value: StepSearch is limited to text-only question answering and small parameter scales.
current_limitations.evidence: Evaluation has been restricted to text-only question answering, leaving open the question of how well the approach generalizes to multimodal inputs.

future_research_directions.value: Future work will need to address gaps to realize a truly generalizable, robust, and scalable retrieval-augmented agent.
future_research_directions.evidence: Future work will need to address these gaps to realize a truly generalizable, robust, and scalable retrieval-augmented agent.

key_contribution.value: The core contribution lies in the development of StepSearch with dual rewards and improved performance on multi-hop QA.
key_contribution.evidence: In general, our core contribution lies in: StepSearch: Step-wise RL with dual rewards.

broader_implications.value: This research impacts AI systems' development by enhancing retrieval-augmented reasoning capabilities.
broader_implications.evidence: This research impacts AI systems' development by enhancing retrieval-augmented reasoning capabilities.
